title: Ranunculus weberbaueri
intro: Ranunculus weberbaueri, also known as Peruvian buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, native to the Andes mountains of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus weberbaueri include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as ranunculin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ranunculus weberbaueri include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Ranunculus weberbaueri is classified within the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. This plant belongs to the genus Ranunculus, which comprises approximately 600 species of flowering plants. The species Ranunculus weberbaueri is native to the Andean region and is often found in mountainous areas. Its classification is further supported by its characteristics, such as its white or yellow flowers and pinnate leaves. As a member of the Ranunculaceae family, Ranunculus weberbaueri shares similarities with other buttercup species.

In traditional medicine, it has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues.
